channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US strikes Iranian radar sites after shooting down drones near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after shooting down drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz, in the latest escalation complicating efforts to end the war between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: Ukraine and Russia exchange 185 prisoners each in latest swap
item.description: Ukraine and Russia each exchanged 185 service personnel in the latest prisoner of war swap, officials in both countries said, continuing regular exchanges despite ongoing hostilities.

item.title: Sea drone self-detonates near Romanian Black Sea port amid Russian jamming
item.description: A sea drone self-destructed near an oil terminal in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta without causing casualties, as Ukraine said Russian jamming caused the vessel to drift off course.

item.title: Russian drone attack kills four at dairy factory in Kyiv region
item.description: A Russian drone attack on a dairy factory in the Ukrainian capital region killed four people and injured seven more, Ukrainian officials said.

item.title: Bank of Canada expected to hold rates through 2026 despite inflation risks
item.description: The Bank of Canada will hold its key overnight rate at 2.25% on June 10 and for the rest of the year, according to a Reuters poll, despite rising inflation risks from conflict-driven energy prices.

item.title: UN Security Council to convene emergency meeting on Russian missile terror
item.description: Ukraine requested an emergency UN Security Council meeting for June 8 in response to Russia recent large-scale ballistic missile attacks on Ukrainian cities. Foreign Minister Sybiha said Moscow continues choosing escalation over peace.

item.title: Iran FM rejects Lebanese interference accusations, says focus on real foe
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi dismissed Lebanese President Joseph Aoun accusation of interference, telling him to focus on the real foe as Lebanon leaders call for Iran to stop interfering in their affairs.

item.title: China mobilizes SOEs and tech firms to hire record 12.7 million graduates
item.description: Eight government departments launched a nationwide campaign requiring state-owned enterprises and major private tech firms to expand hiring for the class of 2026, marking one of China broadest labor market interventions in recent years.

item.title: KMT leader says Taiwan can preserve peace by avoiding de jure independence
item.description: Kuomintang Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun told a Harvard seminar that cross-strait peace can be maintained as long as Taipei does not cross the red line of de jure independence, advocating for both deterrence and dialogue frameworks.

item.title: Armenia heads to polls in election that could reshape ties with Moscow and the West
item.description: Armenia holds parliamentary elections Sunday as Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan seeks closer Western ties while opposition parties favor Russia. The vote could determine Armenia's geopolitical alignment in the South Caucasus.

item.title: Macron, Starmer and Merz to meet Zelenskiy in London for Ukraine talks
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz will hold talks with Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy on Sunday, according to the French presidency.

item.title: Putin says he sees no reason to meet with Zelenskiy currently
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he currently sees no reason to meet with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy, reiterating Moscow's position on negotiations as the conflict continues.

item.title: Iran declares support for Hezbollah with wider peace deal in doubt
item.description: Iran has reaffirmed support for its Lebanese ally Hezbollah and demanded that Israel withdraw from southern Lebanon, underscoring complications facing an interim deal to end the broader conflict between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: Five dead in Russian attacks on Ukraine's southern Kherson region
item.description: Russian attacks on Ukraine's southern Kherson region killed five people, local officials reported, as fighting continues along the front lines.

item.title: Pope Leo heads to Spain with migrants and polarisation in focus
item.description: Pope Leo leaves on Saturday for a week-long visit to Spain, his first to an EU country outside Italy, where he will address migration and social polarisation issues.

item.title: Putin held friendly one-on-one meeting with Germany's Schroeder, Kremlin says
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin held a one-on-one meeting with former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder, which was "good and friendly," Kremlin aide Yuri Ushakov was quoted as saying.

item.title: UK house prices fell unexpectedly in May, Halifax says
item.description: UK house prices fell unexpectedly in May according to Halifax, showing weakness in the housing market amid economic uncertainty.

item.title: Canada adds 87,800 jobs, jobless rate down to 6.6%, beating May estimates
item.description: Canada added 87,800 jobs in May with the unemployment rate falling to 6.6%, beating expectations in a sign of labor market resilience.

item.title: Black Sea attack fishing vessel incident under investigation
item.description: An attack on a fishing vessel in the Black Sea is under investigation as regional tensions continue to affect civilian maritime activity.

item.title: OPEC output plunges further as US squeezes Iran, survey shows
item.description: OPEC oil production fell further as US sanctions continue to squeeze Iranian exports, according to a Bloomberg survey showing declining output from the cartel.

item.title: Iran dodges US blockade through Iraq as new smuggling route emerges
item.description: Iran is using Iraq as a smuggling route to circumvent US blockade efforts, Arab Times reports, as Tehran seeks alternatives to move goods amid intensifying sanctions.

item.title: UAE markets gain despite stalled US-Iran peace talks
item.description: UAE financial markets posted gains despite the stall in US-Iran peace negotiations, showing regional investor confidence amid geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: China bets on AI to promote President Xi Jinping's thinking
item.description: China is deploying artificial intelligence technology to promote President Xi Jinping's political philosophy and governance ideas, extending the Communist Party's propaganda capabilities into the AI era.

item.title: BBC reports on global developments amid ongoing conflicts
item.description: BBC continues coverage of ongoing global developments as multiple conflicts and diplomatic efforts reshape international relations.

item.title: Iran-Israel war live updates: Strait of Hormuz tensions, Lebanon, uranium developments
item.description: FirstPost provides continuing coverage of the Iran-Israel conflict including developments around the Strait of Hormuz, the Lebanon front, and uranium enrichment activities.
